examples.title: HDLC Bit Stuffing Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to perform bit stuffing on data before transmission
examples.code:
FUNCTION bitStuff(data):
 stuffed = ""
 count = 0
 FOR each bit in data:
 stuffed = stuffed + bit
 IF bit == '1':
 count = count + 1
 ELSE:
 count = 0
 IF count == 5:
 stuffed = stuffed + '0'
 count = 0
 RETURN stuffed
examples.explanation: The sender inserts a '0' after every five consecutive '1' bits to prevent the flag sequence (01111110) from appearing in the data stream.

examples.title: HDLC Frame Reception and Destuffing
examples.description: Algorithm to destuff received data and verify frame integrity
examples.code:
FUNCTION destuffAndValidate(stuffedData):
 data = ""
 count = 0
 FOR each bit in stuffedData:
 IF bit == '1':
 count = count + 1
 ELSE:
 count = 0
 data = data + bit
 IF count == 5:
 # Skip the next '0' (stuffed bit)
 count = 0
 ELSE:
 IF bit == '0':
 data = data + bit
 RETURN data
examples.explanation: The receiver removes '0' bits that follow five consecutive '1' bits, restoring the original data stream.

examples.title: HDLC FCS Generation (CRC-CCITT)
examples.description: Algorithm to generate 16-bit Frame Check Sequence
examples.code:
FUNCTION generateFCS(data):
 # CRC-CCITT polynomial: x^16 + x^12 + x^5 + 1
 polynomial = 0x1021
 crc = 0xFFFF # Initial value
 FOR each byte in data:
 crc = crc XOR (byte << 8)
 FOR i = 0 to 7:
 IF (crc & 0x8000) != 0:
 crc = (crc << 1) XOR polynomial
 ELSE:
 crc = crc << 1
 crc = crc AND 0xFFFF
 RETURN crc
examples.explanation: FCS uses CRC-CCITT polynomial for robust error detection. Both sender and receiver compute FCS; mismatch indicates transmission error.

examples.title: HDLC Sliding Window Protocol
examples.description: Algorithm for managing send and receive windows in HDLC
examples.code:
CONSTANT WINDOW_SIZE = 7
FUNCTION sendFrame(seqNum, data):
 IF (seqNum - lastAckedSeq) < WINDOW_SIZE:
 frame = createFrame(seqNum, data)
 send(frame)
 startTimer(seqNum)
 RETURN TRUE
 ELSE:
 RETURN FALSE # Window full, must wait

FUNCTION handleAck(receiveSeq):
 IF receiveSeq > lastAckedSeq:
 stopTimer(lastAckedSeq)
 lastAckedSeq = receiveSeq
 RETURN TRUE
 RETURN FALSE

FUNCTION handleTimeout(seqNum):
 # Go-back-N: retransmit from seqNum
 FOR i = seqNum to lastSentSeq:
 retransmit(i)
examples.explanation: HDLC uses sliding window protocol for efficient data transfer. Send window controls unacknowledged frames; receive window accepts in-order frames.
